name: Sifnos
subtitle: The gourmet Cycladic island — pottery, chickpea soup and the finest food in the archipelago
intro: Sifnos has a reputation entirely disproportionate to its size — the best food in the Cyclades, a tradition of pottery going back 4000 years, a string of beautiful Venetian villages, and excellent beaches. The slow-cooked revithada (chickpea soup, cooked overnight in a ceramic pot in the village bakery) is the local dish. The island rewards slow travel — walk the old mule-path network between villages rather than driving.

itinerary.title: 3-Day Sifnos Itinerary
itinerary.subtitle: Apollonia's lanes, the hilltop monastery of Chrysopigi, the beaches of Platis Gialos and the pottery village of Artemonas.

stops.name: Apollonia
stops.desc: The capital — a beautiful hilltop town of white lanes, blue-domed churches and bougainvillea. Walk the main pedestrian street to Artemonas. The folk museum is excellent.

stops.name: Artemonas
stops.desc: The most elegant village on Sifnos — Venetian mansions and the finest pottery on the island. Several workshops sell hand-thrown Sifniot ceramics in the traditional geometric patterns.

stops.name: Kastro (Medieval Capital)
stops.desc: The medieval capital of Sifnos — a Venetian fortress-village on a sea cliff with extraordinary views. The houses form the outer wall. The Archaeological Museum inside is small but excellent.

stops.name: Platis Gialos Beach
stops.desc: The best beach on the island — long, sandy, shallow and well-organised. The most popular beach on Sifnos but large enough to never feel overcrowded.

stops.name: Chrysopigi Monastery
stops.desc: The most beautiful monastery in the Cyclades — a 17th-century whitewashed complex on a tiny rocky peninsula connected by a causeway. The setting is extraordinary.

stops.name: Apokofto Beach
stops.desc: Sandy beach directly below the Chrysopigi monastery — the monastery visible on the rocks above makes this one of the most picturesque beach settings in the Cyclades.

stops.name: Faros Village
stops.desc: Traditional fishing village with three small coves and the best fish tavernas on the island. Lunch here — the grilled fish and local white wine are outstanding.

stops.name: Vathy Beach
stops.desc: Remote sandy beach in a deep bay on the west coast — the most unspoiled beach on Sifnos, accessible by a long winding road. Beautiful and quiet.

stops.name: Kato Petali to Kastro Walk
stops.desc: The finest walk on Sifnos — the old mule path from Apollonia to Kastro through olive groves and Cycladic chapels. 45 minutes, flat and beautiful.

stops.name: Kamares Beach
stops.desc: The port beach — long, sandy and sheltered in the harbour bay. Good facilities and a lively waterfront with cafes. The best beach for people-watching.

stops.name: Apollonia — Departure
stops.desc: Ferry from Kamares port to Piraeus, Milos, Serifos or Paros.

beaches.name: Platis Gialos Beach
beaches.facilities: Well organised: sunbeds, beach bars, tavernas, water sports.
beaches.desc: The best beach on Sifnos — long, sandy and shallow with excellent facilities. The most popular on the island but large enough to absorb the crowds. The tavernas at the back of the beach serve outstanding Sifniot food.

beaches.name: Apokofto Beach
beaches.facilities: Basic: the Chrysopigi monastery taverna nearby. Beautiful setting.
beaches.desc: The most photogenic beach in the Cyclades — fine sand directly below the whitewashed Chrysopigi monastery on its rocky peninsula. The setting is extraordinary. One of those beaches where the surroundings matter as much as the swimming.

beaches.name: Vathy Beach
beaches.facilities: Basic: small taverna, limited sunbeds. Remote — long winding road access.
beaches.desc: The most remote and peaceful beach on Sifnos — a sandy cove in a deep bay on the west coast. The long drive keeps it quiet. A small taverna, calm water and beautiful surroundings.
